gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A registry operation refusal — always explicit, never a partial/silent result (G2)."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq , Eq)] pub enum RegistryError { # [doc = " An invalid input: a missing/empty version label, or a `name`/`version` that is not a safe"] # [doc = " path component (used by both `publish` and `resolve`) (exit 3)."] InvalidInput (String) , # [doc = " A `name`/`version` with no index entry (exit 4)."] NotFound (String) , # [doc = " A content-integrity failure: the object is absent, or its bytes don't hash to the recorded"] # [doc = " `artifact`, or a recorded address is malformed (exit 5)."] Integrity (String) , # [doc = " An immutability violation: a different artifact already occupies this `name@version` (exit 6)."] Conflict (String) , # [doc = " A version *constraint* v0 cannot honestly satisfy (a range/caret/tilde) (exit 64)."] Unsupported (String) , # [doc = " An I/O error touching the registry tree (exit 66)."] Io (String) , }

gaps.snippet:
# [doc = " Reject a `name`/`version` that is not a **safe single path component**, so it can never escape the"] # [doc = " registry root (path traversal). `[project].name`/`version` are author-controlled strings that"] # [doc = " `mycelium-proj` does not constrain to path-safe forms, so the registry validates them itself here:"] # [doc = " empty, `.`, `..`, or any string containing `/`, `\\`, or a NUL is refused — never silently joined"] # [doc = " into a path (G2). This guards `publish`, `resolve`, and `select_version`."] fn safe_component (kind : & str , value : & str) -> Result < () , RegistryError > { let bad = value . is_empty () || value == "." || value == ".." || value . contains ('/') || value . contains ('\\') || value . contains ('\0') ; if bad { return Err (RegistryError :: InvalidInput (format ! ("{kind} {value:?} is not a safe path component — a registry {kind} may not be empty, `.`, \
             `..`, or contain `/`, `\\`, or NUL (refusing to escape the registry root; G2)"))) ; } Ok (()) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " **Resolve** `name` at `constraint` against the registry at `root`, returning the integrity-verified"] # [doc = " artifact. `constraint` is either an **exact version** (`\"1.2.0\"`) or `\"latest\"` / `\"*\"` (the"] # [doc = " highest published version). A *range* constraint (`^`, `~`, `>=`) is [`RegistryError::Unsupported`]"] # [doc = " — v0 never silently mis-resolves a SemVer range it cannot honestly evaluate (the range backend is"] # [doc = " the deferred ADR-018 work)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = " [`RegistryError::NotFound`] if no matching `name`/version exists; [`RegistryError::Unsupported`]"] # [doc = " for a range constraint; [`RegistryError::Integrity`] if the object is missing or its bytes fail"] # [doc = " the `artifact` hash check; [`RegistryError::Io`] on a filesystem failure."] pub fn resolve (root : & Path , name : & str , constraint : & str) -> Result < Resolved , RegistryError > { safe_component ("name" , name) ? ; let version = select_version (root , name , constraint) ? ; let idx = index_path (root , name , & version) ; let entry = std :: fs :: read_to_string (& idx) . map_err (| _ | { RegistryError :: NotFound (format ! ("{name}@{version} has no registry index entry")) }) ? ; let (spore_id , artifact) = parse_entry (& entry) . map_err (| e | RegistryError :: Integrity (format ! ("{} — {e}" , idx . display ()))) ? ; let obj = object_path (root , & artifact) ; let bytes = std :: fs :: read (& obj) . map_err (| _ | { RegistryError :: Integrity (format ! ("{name}@{version} index points at a missing object {} (store corruption)" , obj . display ())) }) ? ; if artifact_hash (& bytes) != artifact { return Err (RegistryError :: Integrity (format ! ("{name}@{version}: fetched object {} does not match its content address {} — tampered or corrupt" , obj . display () , artifact . as_str ()))) ; } Ok (Resolved { name : name . to_owned () , version , spore_id , artifact , bytes , }) }
gaps.reason: function body has a semicolon-terminated (value-discarding) statement expression before the tail — a `let`-chain body maps only simple `let x = e;` bindings plus a single trailing expression

gaps.snippet:
# [doc = " Choose the concrete version for `constraint`: an exact match, or the highest published version for"] # [doc = " `latest`/`*`. A range constraint is refused (unsupported in v0; never silently mis-resolved)."] fn select_version (root : & Path , name : & str , constraint : & str) -> Result < String , RegistryError > { let c = constraint . trim () ; if c . is_empty () { return Err (RegistryError :: NotFound (format ! ("{name}: an empty version constraint resolves nothing (it is never guessed)"))) ; } if c == "latest" || c == "*" { let mut versions = published_versions (root , name) ? ; versions . sort_by_key (| v | version_key (v)) ; return versions . pop () . ok_or_else (| | RegistryError :: NotFound (format ! ("{name}: no versions published"))) ; } if c . starts_with (['^' , '~' , '>' , '<' , '=']) || c . contains (',') { return Err (RegistryError :: Unsupported (format ! ("version constraint {c:?} is a range — v0 resolves an exact version or `latest` only; \
             SemVer range resolution is the deferred ADR-018 work, not silently approximated (VR-5)"))) ; } safe_component ("version" , c) ? ; if index_path (root , name , c) . exists () { Ok (c . to_owned ()) } else { Err (RegistryError :: NotFound (format ! ("{name}@{c} is not published"))) } }

Emitting bare `trim(recv, …)` would fabricate an unknown prim and file-poison myc-check (G-β Rank A / G2/VR-5; residual when the method body itself gapped — e.g. M-1037 `.to_vec()` inside `read_to_end` — so the declaration was never registered)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The published version labels for `name` (the filenames under `index/<name>/`)."] fn published_versions (root : & Path , name : & str) -> Result < Vec < String > , RegistryError > { let dir = root . join ("index") . join (name) ; let rd = std :: fs :: read_dir (& dir) . map_err (| _ | RegistryError :: NotFound (format ! ("{name}: not published in this registry"))) ? ; let mut out = Vec :: new () ; for e in rd { let e = e . map_err (| err | io (& dir . display () . to_string () , err)) ? ; if e . path () . is_file () { if let Some (v) = e . file_name () . to_str () { out . push (v . to_owned ()) ; } } } Ok (out) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A coarse version sort key: dotted numeric components compared numerically, with a lexical"] # [doc = " fallback for non-numeric parts. Honest scope (`Declared`): this orders simple `MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH`"] # [doc = " labels for `latest`; it is **not** a full SemVer precedence implementation (pre-release/build"] # [doc = " metadata are not interpreted) — that is the deferred ADR-018 work."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `pub(crate)` so [`crate::remote::resolve_remote`] reuses the same `latest`-selection ordering"] # [doc = " instead of re-deriving it (DRY; ADR-037 §2 explicitly asks for reuse here)."] pub (crate) fn version_key (v : & str) -> Vec < (u64 , String) > { v . split ('.') . map (| part | { let num = part . chars () . take_while (char :: is_ascii_digit) . collect :: < String > () ; (num . parse :: < u64 > () . unwrap_or (0) , part . to_owned ()) }) . collect () }

("spore_id = {}\nartifact = {}\n" , spore_id . as_str () , artifact . as_str ()) }
gaps.reason: Rust ownership/identity-conversion no-op method `.as_str()` has no Mycelium free-function/prim referent (value semantics — ADR-003); desugaring it to a bare `as_str(recv)` would fabricate an unknown prim (`unknown function/constructor/prim `as_str`` — verified against the oracle), so it is gapped, never fake-emitted (G2/VR-5)

gaps.snippet:
# [doc = " Parse a [`format_entry`] index entry back into `(spore_id, artifact)`. **Total and strict**: every"] # [doc = " line must be either blank or a recognized `key = value` (`spore_id`/`artifact`), and a key may occur"] # [doc = " at most **once**. On any malformed/missing/duplicate/unrecognized field this returns an explicit"] # [doc = " [`RegistryError::Integrity`] **naming the offending line/key** — never a silent default, never a"] # [doc = " silent last-wins, never a silently-ignored stray line (G2). A corrupt or hand-edited index entry is"] # [doc = " thus caught at read-back, not papered over."] # [doc = " `pub(crate)` for white-box in-crate test access (CLAUDE.md test-layout rule); not a public API."] pub (crate) fn parse_entry (text : & str) -> Result < (ContentHash , ContentHash) , RegistryError > { let mut spore_id = None ; let mut artifact = None ; for line in text . lines () { if line . trim () . is_empty () { continue ; } let Some ((k , v)) = line . split_once ('=') else { return Err (RegistryError :: Integrity (format ! ("malformed index entry: line {line:?} is neither blank nor a `key = value` pair \
                 (refusing to silently ignore an unrecognized line; G2)"))) ; } ; let key = k . trim () ; let value = v . trim () ; let slot = match key { "spore_id" => & mut spore_id , "artifact" => & mut artifact , other => { return Err (RegistryError :: Integrity (format ! ("malformed index entry: unrecognized key {other:?} in line {line:?} \
                     (expected `spore_id` or `artifact`; refusing to silently ignore it; G2)"))) ; } } ; if slot . is_some () { return Err (RegistryError :: Integrity (format ! ("malformed index entry: duplicate `{key}` line {line:?} — a key occurs at most once \
                 (refusing last-wins; G2)"))) ; } let parsed = ContentHash :: parse (value) . ok_or_else (| | { RegistryError :: Integrity (format ! ("malformed index entry: `{key}` value {value:?} is not a valid content hash (G2)")) }) ? ; * slot = Some (parsed) ; } let spore_id = spore_id . ok_or_else (| | { RegistryError :: Integrity ("malformed index entry: missing `spore_id` line (G2)" . to_owned ()) }) ? ; let artifact = artifact . ok_or_else (| | { RegistryError :: Integrity ("malformed index entry: missing `artifact` line (G2)" . to_owned ()) }) ? ; Ok ((spore_id , artifact)) }
